outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Houthis Fire First Missile at Israel as War Spreads to Yemen
outlets.summary: CNN coverage focuses on Israeli cluster munition use and civilian death, nuclear escalation at Arak and Ardakan, and Trump threatening NATO allies over Hormuz.

outlets.name: MSNBC / The Washington Post
outlets.headline: Trump's 'Paper Tiger' NATO Remarks Deepen Transatlantic Rift
outlets.summary: WaPo/NPR reporting that US intelligence can only verify destruction of one-third of Iran's missile arsenal, undercutting Trump's claims of decisive wins.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Russia Is Helping Iran Target and Kill Americans, EU's Top Diplomat Says at G7
outlets.summary: CNN highlights the Kallas bombshell at G7 — Russia providing intelligence to Iran — and the US-EU divergence on whether to believe Russia's denial.

outlets.name: NBC News
outlets.headline: Stocks Fall, Oil Prices Rise Amid Doubts Over US-Iran Talks
outlets.summary: NBC reports S&P 500 fell 1.74% — worst day of the war — as Iran rejected the 15-point peace plan; Brent rose to .99, WTI .48.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump Warns NATO Allies: Send Warships or Lose US Protection
outlets.summary: Fox frames the G7 meeting as the US carrying the burden while European allies offer only weak support for the conflict.

outlets.name: Wall Street Journal
outlets.headline: Rubio: Iran War Ends in 'Weeks, Not Months' — No Ground Troops Needed
outlets.summary: WSJ reports Trump privately telling aides he wants to conclude the conflict before his planned China trip in mid-May 2026.

outlets.name: NY Post
outlets.headline: Hegseth: 'Iran Has No Navy, No Navy Leader — Pure American Success'
outlets.summary: Conservative media amplifies Hegseth's triumphalist framing after IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri's killing, with 92% of Iran's large navy eliminated.

outlets.name: Daily Caller
outlets.headline: Pentagon Planning to Deploy 10,000 More Troops — 82nd Airborne Headed to Middle East
outlets.summary: Right-leaning outlets frame the 10,000-troop deployment planning as showing US resolve to finish the war on its terms.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: War on Iran: Five-Front Conflict as Houthis Enter with Missile Attack on Israel
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera leads with Iranian vow of retaliation and Araghchi's explicit denial of peace talks, framing the nuclear strikes as a contradiction of Trump's diplomatic pause.

outlets.name: BBC World Service
outlets.headline: Pakistan Hosts Emergency Summit — Saudi, Turkey, Egypt Push for Iran Ceasefire
outlets.summary: BBC leads with Kallas's explosive claim that Russia is feeding Iran intelligence to kill Americans, including satellite imagery and drone technology upgrades.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: Russia Is Giving Iran Intel to Kill Americans, Kallas Tells G7 — UK Confirms
outlets.summary: BBC and international outlets lead with the Russia-Iran intelligence sharing revelation; UK Defense Secretary confirms with British intelligence data.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Brent Crude Hits  as Iran Rejects Peace Plan; 10,000 More US Troops Eyed for Middle East
outlets.summary: Reuters covers markets and military escalation simultaneously — oil at .99 on broken ceasefire hopes, Pentagon actively planning major ground troop deployment.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Yemen Joins the War: First Houthi Missile at Israel Intercepted
outlets.summary: Times of Israel comprehensive Day 28 coverage focuses on nuclear escalation, industrial strikes, 2 IDF killed in Lebanon, and cluster bomb civilian death.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: RUSI: Israel Running Low on Arrow Interceptors; UK Declines US Interceptor Request
outlets.summary: Haaretz leads with RUSI report on interceptor depletion and intelligence assessment that Mossad's promised Iranian uprising has not materialized.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Netanyahu Ordered IDF to Maximize Iran Strikes in 48 Hours Before Possible Ceasefire
outlets.summary: Haaretz reports Netanyahu and security chiefs do not trust Trump's 15-point plan to remove the nuclear/missile threat; IDF ordered to accelerate arms factory targeting.

outlets.name: Ynet
outlets.headline: Iran Wave 83: Four US Gulf Bases Struck Simultaneously; Yazd Naval Arms Site Destroyed by IAF
outlets.summary: Israeli media covering the expanded geographic scope of IRGC attacks and IDF's counterstrike degrading Iran's naval production capacity.

outlets.name: PressTV (Iranian state)
outlets.headline: IRGC Strikes Kuwait Airport, UAE, Oman in Coordinated Gulf Operation
outlets.summary: PressTV frames IRGC Wave 84 and evacuation warning as Iran's assertive defense, presenting Araghchi's rejection of negotiations as strength.

outlets.name: IRNA (Iranian state news)
outlets.headline: Araghchi: Israel Will Pay Heavy Price for Nuclear Facility Crimes
outlets.summary: IRNA leads with Araghchi's framing of Israeli nuclear strikes as contradicting US diplomatic claims, demanding heavy reparations.

outlets.name: Tehran Times
outlets.headline: Araghchi: US Admitting Defeat by Seeking Negotiations — Iran's Conditions Non-Negotiable
outlets.summary: State media amplifies Araghchi's framing that US seeking talks proves American defeat, and Iran's 5 counter-conditions are firm.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Islamic Republic Forces Continue Operation True Promise 4 — Striking Enemy Infrastructure
outlets.summary: State news agency covers IRGC Wave 83 as defensive resistance against US-Israeli aggression; claims strikes damaged Patriot systems at US bases.
